title: Conway's Game of Life
description: A cellular automata simulation implementing Conway's Game of Life with double-buffered 2D arrays, toroidal wrapping, click-drag cell editing, preset blueprints (Glider, Pulsar, Acorn, Gosper Gun), FPS/scale controls, and localStorage custom preset persistence.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Double-buffered 2D arrays: current/next grids hold boolean cell states; step() computes next from current via 8-neighbor toroidal convolution, then atomic swap
Toroidal neighbor counting: (x % n + n) % n wrapping ensures seamless edge traversal for all cells

Canvas pixel rendering: alive cells drawn as filled squares (cellSize-2 padding), newborn (#00f0ff with cyan glow) vs survivor (#6a5acd with violet glow) color differentiation using stale nextGrid state
Click-drag editing: mousedown captures paintValue (inverse of clicked cell), mousemove paints same value, mouseup ends; touch toggle on tap
FPS-throttled loop: requestAnimationFrame with manual interval gating (1000/fps), step+draw only when elapsed >= interval
Preset blueprints: Glider (5 cells), Pulsar (48-cell period-3 oscillator), Acorn (7-cell R-pentomino), Gosper Gun (36-cell glider factory) — centered via offset calculation
localStorage custom presets: saveBtn serializes alive cells as compact JSON [c,r] array, stored under cgol_custom_timestamp key, loaded into dropdown on init
Telemetry: generation counter, live cell tally, population delta (liveCount - prevLive) updated each step
Scale/FPS sliders: scale (3-20px) triggers recalcGrid() preserving alive cells, FPS (1-30) controls simulation speed
